Wire up the existing VNext SPA infrastructure that was built but never

BMW WebSocket Transport in BareMetalRest.js:
- Add connectWs() — opens WebSocket to /bmw/ws with binary frames

- _tryWs() attempts WebSocket dispatch, returns null if unavailable
- Expose connectWs(), isWsReady() in public API

Tests:

Scale difficulty via classic sequence repetition (option 2)

Replace snake-generated random boards with four classic-sequence
variants of increasing length. The 1-9 repeating sequence creates
genuine forced dependencies — clearing one number unlocks others
rather than every pair being a direct neighbor.

Variants (all at 6 columns wide):
  Intro    1-9 x 2  = 18 cells,  3 initial valid pairs
  Standard 1-9 x 4  = 36 cells,  7 initial valid pairs
  Hard     1-9 x 6  = 54 cells, 11 initial valid pairs
  Expert   1-9 x 8  = 72 cells, 15 initial valid pairs

Removed: generateByConstruction, buildWorker, generateRandom,
Web Worker, activeWorker, genStatus/genAttempt UI elements.
Board creation is now instant and synchronous.

* Fix TextValidateField test failures and TimeEditor EnableForDesign crash

Revert breaking changes to TextValidateField that were introduced for
TimeEditor but broke existing TVF tests: Width+1, EndKeyHandler+1,
CursorRight past end, mouse click past CursorEnd, OnKeyDownNotHandled
rewrite, BackspaceKeyHandler, and GetMargins End alignment.

InvalidCastException when AllViewsTests calls EnableForDesign.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix TimeEditor cursor/insertion by normalizing time format patterns

Single-digit format specifiers (h, H, m, s) caused variable-width
fields where cursor positions shifted based on the current time value.
Typing "12" at position 0 of a single-digit hour would place "1" in
hours and "2" in minutes because CursorRight skipped the separator
at position 1.

Normalize all format specifiers to 2-digit versions (hh, HH, mm, ss)
so field positions are always consistent. Remove the leading space
hack from DisplayText since padding is no longer needed.

Add 9 unit tests covering cursor navigation, insertion at all digit
positions, separator skipping, and DriverAssert rendering verification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Improve TimeEditor width and TextValidateField cursor logic

- TimeEditor now sets width to fit full DisplayText (incl. AM/PM).
- TextValidateField allows cursor one past last editable char.
- Fixed width calculation for fixed fields (DisplayText.Length + 1).
- Updated tests for new width and cursor behavior.
- Added labels in TimeAndDate demo to show time patterns.
- Added tests to verify full AM/PM visibility and width logic.
- Improves usability and prevents clipping of time strings.

* Improve cursor handling at end of masked text fields

Enhances navigation and editing in masked fields (TextValidateField, TimeEditor) to prevent the cursor from moving backward when pressing right arrow from the blank cell past the last editable character. Backspace from the blank cell now deletes the last editable character. TimeEditor minimum width is increased by one to always allow a blank cell. Updates logic for right-arrow navigation in fixed providers. Adds unit tests to verify correct cursor and editing behavior at the end of input. These changes address subtle usability issues and make cursor movement more intuitive.

* Replace TimeField with new TimeEditor control

Removed the TimeField control, its tests, and documentation. Introduced TimeEditor as the new time input control, based on TextValidateField with culture-aware formatting. Updated all references, scenarios, and docs to use TimeEditor instead of TimeField. TimeField source and tests have been deleted.

* Refactor TimeEditor and providers, remove VerifyChar method

Refactored TimeEditor, TimeTextProvider, NetMaskedTextProvider, and TextRegexProvider for clarity and consistency. Removed the VerifyChar method from ITextValidateProvider and all implementations, simplifying the provider interface. Standardized event raising and improved handling of AM/PM, manual parsing, and pattern normalization in TimeTextProvider. Updated TextValidateField drawing and key handling logic for simplicity. Modernized and clarified all related tests to match the new API and style. Made various code style improvements and updated documentation throughout.

* Implement CWP and IValue<T> for TextValidateField/TimeEditor

Refactored TextValidateField and TimeEditor to fully implement the IValue<T> interface and follow the Cancellable Work Pattern (CWP) for value changes.
- TextValidateField now exposes Value, ValueChanging, and ValueChanged events, and synchronizes value changes from both programmatic and user input, with support for cancellation and reversion.
- Introduced SuppressValueEvents to prevent unwanted event recursion.
- TimeEditor now uses a private _value field, leverages CWPPropertyHelper for value changes, and raises strongly-typed TimeSpan events.
- Removed manual event wiring and last-known-value tracking in favor of the new base class pattern.
- These changes improve consistency, extensibility, and support for data binding and undo/redo scenarios.

* Replace DateField with new DateEditor based on TextValidateField

Add DateTextProvider (ITextValidateProvider for dates with culture-aware
formatting, pattern normalization, separator handling) and DateEditor
(extends TextValidateField, implements IValue<DateTime?> with CWP).
Remove legacy DateField and update all usages in DatePicker, scenarios,
docs, and indexes. Full test coverage in DateEditorTests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* code cleanup

* code cleanup

* Refactor TextChanged event handling for providers

Refactored event raising in Date, Time, Masked, and Regex text providers by introducing a private RaiseTextChanged method. OnTextChanged is now virtual and serves as a subclass hook, improving extensibility. Updated all event triggers to use RaiseTextChanged. Improved null safety in TextRegexProvider by initializing _text to an empty list. Fixed CleanSeparator logic in DateTextProvider for short separators. Enhanced TryParseDateValue to only fall back to manual parsing if needed. Updated documentation to clarify new event handling pattern.

feat: strict mode, WeakRef, Object.prototype.toString conformance (#449)

* Enforce strict mode semantics for undeclared vars, this binding, params, and octals

- StaGlobal/StaLookupSlot: throw ReferenceError when assigning to
  undeclared variables in strict mode instead of creating globals
- CallUndefinedReceiver0/1/2: set 'this' to undefined for strict
  callee functions (save/restore around shared global_env)
- Parser: reject duplicate simple parameter names in strict mode
  functions (checked after body parse so 'use strict' directives work)
- Parser: reject legacy octal literals (e.g. 0123) in strict mode
  (0o123 is still allowed)
- Scanner: expose source() getter for raw token text inspection
- Existing 'with' statement and 'delete variable' checks already present

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(builtins): use Weak<T> for WeakRef/FinalizationRegistry PlainObject targets

WeakRef:
- Add WeakTarget enum supporting both Rc-managed (Weak<RefCell<PropertyMap>>)
  and GC-managed (*mut HeapObject) targets
- Add weak_ref_new_plain() for PlainObject targets using Rc::downgrade()
- deref() now returns Undefined automatically when Rc target is collected
- Constructor in install_globals accepts both Object and PlainObject

FinalizationRegistry:
- Add PlainRegistration with Weak<RefCell<PropertyMap>> target tracking
- Add finalization_registry_register_plain() for PlainObject targets
- Add finalization_registry_unregister_plain() for PlainObject tokens
- Add finalization_registry_sweep_plain() to detect collected Rc targets

commit.message:
fix(orleans): R4 deep audit — grain call filter error/histogram metrics, stale position pruning, gradient age eviction

Fix 1: Grain call filter — added goldaig.silo.grain_errors counter with
grain.type/error.type tags and grain_call_duration histogram for p50/p95/p99
latency percentiles. Previously errors had zero OTel metrics.

Fix 2: RiskManagementGrain — added OpenedAtUtc to RiskOpenPosition with
schema v2 migration, plus 30-min timer-based stale position pruning (>7 days).
Prevents phantom positions from API disconnects accumulating below 2x cap.

Fix 3: MLTrainingGrain — added age-based gradient eviction (>24h) on
activation and during SubmitGradientsAsync. Stale gradients from inactive
contributors no longer pollute FedAvg aggregation under the 1000-entry FIFO cap.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Easy Mode: idle pulse hint + selection match lines

When Easy Mode is active (toggle button in controls):

- Idle hint: after 4 seconds of no interaction, the first cell
  of a valid pair pulses with a cyan glow animation. Resets on
  any touch/click.

- Selection lines: when a cell is selected (first click), dashed
  semi-transparent cyan SVG lines are drawn from it to every
  valid matching partner, showing all possible moves.

- Idle hint lines: when the idle hint fires and nothing is
  selected, subtle lines are drawn between ALL currently valid
  pairs at once, giving a broader overview hint.

- resetIdleTimer() called after every interaction and after each
  match animation completes, keeping hint timing accurate.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(esoteric): R4 deep audit — VoC 360° edge normalization, hot-path influence skip, O(1) planet name array

VoC: normalize moonPos to [0,360) before sign computation — (int)(360.0/30.0)=12
produces invalid zodiac sign index and signEnd=390, breaking the applying-aspect
range check at the Pisces/Aries boundary (false VoC positive -> -5.0 penalty).

Hot-path: gate PlanetaryInfluences init (7 writes), per-aspect CollectionsMarshal
mutations (~24/call), name lookups (~24/call), and Mercury/VoC influence writes
behind computeInterpretation flag. Eliminates ~5.3M dict ops per 220K-candle
backtest on the EsotericFeatureProvider hot path.

Planet names: replace PlanetNames.GetValueOrDefault FrozenDictionary hash probes
with flat PlanetNamesByIdFast[planetId] array (O(1) indexing) for cold-path
usages (interpretation, debug logging).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(*): add bootstrap step to `copilot-instructions.md` (#517)

The Copilot instructions did not tell agents to bootstrap the workspace
before running validation or making package changes. In a fresh clone,
that leads to avoidable failures and wasted setup time.

- **Summary**
- Add explicit bootstrap guidance to `.github/copilot-instructions.md`.
- Place it directly before the existing test/build/lint workflow so the
install requirement is seen before any validation commands.

- **Changes**
  - Introduce a new `Bootstrapping` section in the Copilot instructions.
- Require `npm install` from the repo root before tests, builds, lint,
or implementation work.
- Clarify that work should not start until install completes
successfully because workspace dependencies are shared across the
monorepo.

- **Resulting guidance**
  ```md
  ## Bootstrapping

- In a fresh clone, run `npm install` from the repo root before
attempting tests, builds, lint, or package changes.
- Do not start validation or implementation work until the install has
completed successfully, because the workspace dependencies are required
across the monorepo.
  ```

and compute `effective_ids = group_ids or [group_id, "__global__"]`.

pass `effective_ids=effective_ids` in params.

both `$group_id` and `$global_group_id="__global__"` separately (vector

all inline `group_id` Cypher variables renamed to `effective_ids` and
all `= group_id` changed to `IN effective_ids`.

- get_entities_by_importance

commit.message:
fix(engine): R3 deep audit — clamp ML WinProbability [0,1], cap WS reconnect recovery cycles, NaN-guard ensemble disagreement

1. EnsemblePredictionService: Clamp WinProbability to [0,1] at source for
   FastTree, LightGBM, LogisticRegression. ML.NET gradient-boosted models
   can output uncalibrated scores, producing ModelConfidence > 1.0 and
   distorting dynamic weights / agreement scores.

2. CapitalComWebSocketService: Add MAX_RECOVERY_CYCLES (5) with escalating
   delay (2/4/8/16/32 min). Previously, after 5 retries failed, recovery
   looped infinitely at fixed 2-min intervals. Reset cycle counter on
   successful connection.

3. EnsemblePredictionService: Add NaN/Infinity guards to
   CalculateUncertaintyWeightedPrediction and CalculateEnsembleDisagreement.
   Unlike AggregateVotes, these public methods consumed WinProbability raw,
   allowing NaN to propagate through .Average() and variance computation.

commit.message:
Auto-publish on push to main and block major bumps (#58)

## Summary
- Rename `publish.yml` → `cd.yml`, triggered on push to main instead of
GitHub Release
- CD pipeline runs full CI (build + test) before version bump and
publish
- Publish is conditional on actual version bump (`has_bump`), so pushes
with no pending changes skip publishing
- CI becomes PR-only since CD handles main
- Block `major` version bumps in CI (we're not ready for 1.0.0)

## Infinite loop prevention
The version bump commit message includes `[skip ci]`, which GitHub
Actions natively respects to skip all workflows.

## Test plan
- [ ] Merge a PR with a `patch` change file → CD bumps version →
publishes → `[skip ci]` commit doesn't re-trigger
- [ ] Merge a PR with no change files → CD builds/tests but skips
publish
- [ ] PR with a `major` change file → CI blocks it before merge
- [ ] CI no longer runs on push to main

---------

fix: delegate runtime actions to CommandService in DataCommandHandler (#1346)

Runtime-defined entities (e.g. the todo sample) register actions with
Method=null in RemoteCommandMetadata. DataCommandHandler previously
attempted to invoke this null MethodInfo via reflection, throwing a
NullReferenceException that was silently swallowed by a bare catch.

Now when cmd.Method is null the handler delegates to CommandService
which properly resolves the RuntimeActionModel, expands commands via
the ActionExpander, and applies field mutations.

Also surfaces exception messages in both DataCommandHandler and
ActionApiHandlers catch blocks instead of generic 'internal error'.

Fixes #1337
